USAGE:
    goog2kml [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    goog2kml parse -f=C:\downloads\myplaces.csv 
-f=C:\downloads\myfavoriteplaces.csv -o=MyCombinedPlaces.kml
    goog2kml split -f=C:\downloads\myplaces.kml -o=OutputDir/MyPlaces/

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help       Prints help information   
    -v, --version    Prints version information

COMMANDS:
    parse    Parses .csv files generated from a google maps export of Saved     
             Places                                                             
    split    Splits an existing KML file into sub files

Take this GOOGLE for not making this easy 

DESCRIPTION:
Parses .csv files generated from a google maps export of Saved Places

USAGE:
    goog2kml parse [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    goog2kml parse -f=C:\downloads\myplaces.csv 
-f=C:\downloads\myfavoriteplaces.csv -o=MyCombinedPlaces.kml

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help                   Prints help information                        
    -v, --version                Prints version information                     
        --noheader               If true. Does not display the banner on command
                                 execute                                        
        --stopOnError            If true. Stops parsing on any csv row error    
    -v, --verbose                If true. Increases the level of the output     
        --trace                  If true. Outputs all the tracing for each      
                                 processing line                                
        --dryrun                 If true. Runs through the files and estimates  
                                 times to completion                            
    -f, --file <VALUES>          The csv files to parse                         
        --includeComments        If true. Adds any comment from the csv column  
                                 to the description                             
    -s, --stats                  If true. Outputs all the timing stats          
    -p, --parallel               The number of threads used to process Google   
                                 data locations. Default 4                      
    -b, --batch                  The number of items to group into a batch.     
                                 Default 10                                     
    -o, --output                 The output KML file                            
        --placements-per-file    The number of placements to add per KML file.  
                                 Files will be named based on number of files   
                                 needed. Default ALL                            
    -t, --timeout                The timeout to wait on each lookup for         
                                 coordinates from Google. Default 10s

Take this GOOGLE for not making this easy 

DESCRIPTION:
Splits an existing KML file into sub files.

USAGE:
    goog2kml split [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    goog2kml split -f=C:\downloads\myplaces.kml -o=OutputDir/MyPlaces/

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help                   Prints help information                        
    -v, --version                Prints version information                     
        --noheader               If true. Does not display the banner on command
                                 execute                                        
        --stopOnError            If true. Stops parsing on any csv row error    
    -v, --verbose                If true. Increases the level of the output     
        --trace                  If true. Outputs all the tracing for each      
                                 processing line                                
        --dryrun                 If true. Runs through the files and estimates  
                                 times to completion                            
    -f, --file                   The kml file to split                          
    -o, --output                 The output path                                
        --placements-per-file    The number of placements to add per KML file.  
                                 Files will be named based on number of files   
                                 needed. Default ALL
